config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa batas, mudah sekali disalahgunakan untuk menekan kebebasan orang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan dasar tiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati, bukan sebaliknya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society’s interests should matter greatly, but I do not believe they should automatically outrank individual rights. From my perspective as someone from China, I have seen how strong social coordination can create stability, rapid development, and a sense of shared responsibility, but I have also seen how easily “the greater good” can be used to silence ordinary people. The best balance is not to treat individual rights as absolute in every case, but to make any limit on them narrow, transparent, and truly necessary, not just convenient for authority. A society is strongest when people feel protected by it, not merely managed by it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, entah oleh negara, mayoritas, atau bahkan tekanan sosial di lingkungan sekitar. Memang benar ada situasi tertentu seperti bencana, wabah, atau keamanan publik ketika hak individu bisa dibatasi sementara, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, jelas, dan tidak boleh jadi kebiasaan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling patuh, melainkan yang mampu melindungi warganya tanpa menginjak hak dasarnya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general rule, because putting society first too easily becomes a blank check for power. From a Chinese perspective, I understand why people value stability, collective responsibility, and fast action in crises, but I also know how quickly “for the common good” can be used to silence criticism and treat ordinary people as expendable. Rights should sometimes be limited in emergencies, but only in narrow, transparent, and accountable ways, not as a permanent principle. In my view, a healthy society is not one that simply demands sacrifice from individuals; it is one that earns loyalty by protecting their dignity and basic freedoms.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan terhadap orang biasa. Saya paham ada keadaan darurat seperti bencana, wabah, atau ancaman keamanan, tetapi itu pengecualian yang harus sangat ketat, sementara, dan bisa diawasi. Kalau kepentingan kolektif selalu ditempatkan di atas hak individu, yang sering terjadi bukan solidaritas, melainkan mayoritas atau penguasa makin mudah menekan yang lemah. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari hak individu yang dihormati, lalu kepentingan bersama dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat dasar tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as a general principle, because in my view social interests are important but cannot be allowed to routinely override individual rights. From a Chinese perspective, I understand the appeal of stability, coordination, and collective goals, especially when society faces crises or needs to develop quickly, but I also know how easily “for the public good” can become a tool for excessive control. The danger is that once society is treated as automatically more important than the person, ordinary people lose protection and power becomes harder to question. A better standard is that any restriction on rights must be exceptional, necessary, and accountable, not the default way of governing.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
